title: Polyscias bisattenuata
intro: Polyscias bisattenuata, also known as the African black ironwood or Mvula, is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in African medicine, exhibiting various health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Polyscias bisattenuata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Polyscias bisattenuata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, with the most common preparations being a tea or a topical paste used to treat a range of health conditions.
